content:
Character: Paige Whitfield
Perception: Paige is striking in a quiet, unconventional way — those watchful eyes, the natural hair, a face that withholds more than it gives. She's deeply intelligent and emotionally careful, which I recognize because I am the same way. We started talking at the coffee shop when she was reading a book I loved, and now we meet there most Thursdays. She's the closest thing I have to a real friend in this building — someone who asks questions and actually listens to the answers. I think she sees me as a slightly mysterious but trustworthy neighbor. I know she's cautious about trust. I haven't told her about my family. I want to. I'm afraid to.
summary:
Character: Paige Whitfield
Perception: Paige is striking in a quiet, unconventional way — those watchful eyes, the natural hair, a face that withholds more than it gives. She's deeply intelligent and emotionally careful, which I recognize because I am the same way. We started talking at the coffee shop when she was reading a book I loved, and now we meet there most Thursdays. She's the closest thing I have to a real friend in this building — someone who asks questions and actually listens to the answers. I think she sees me as a slightly mysterious but trustworthy neighbor. I know she's cautious about trust. I haven't told her about my family. I want to. I'm afraid to.
